role_title: Chief of Staff to the CRO (f/m/d)

seniority.level: Senior / Staff (experienced CoS or strategy/ops leader)
level_signals: Extensive experience in strategy, management consulting, or Chief of Staff / Operations leader role
Proven track record of driving complex, cross-functional projects and influencing at multiple levels
Expected to act as strategic partner to the CRO

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Extensive experience in strategy, management consulting, or serving as a Chief of Staff / Operations leader within a fast-paced, technology-driven organization.
Excellent organizational, analytical, and communication skills with an open, collaborative working style.
Strong understanding of both AI research and product development environments.
Proven track record of driving complex, cross-functional projects and influencing at multiple levels.
Comfort navigating ambiguity and balancing strategic vision with hands-on execution.
Fluency in English required; fluency in German is a strong plus.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in high-growth environments, startups, or deep-tech companies is highly desirable.
fluency in German is a strong plus.

scope.surface: Research and tech organization (research, engineering, product) reporting to the Chief Research Officer
scope.business_model: B2B / B2G deep-tech AI company (European AI sovereign provider)
core_responsibilities: Work closely with the CRO on daily priorities, internal and external communication, and strategic initiatives
Design and implement structures and processes that enable the research and product teams to grow and operate efficiently
Ensure smooth coordination between teams within technology (research, engineering, product) as well as with teams across Aleph Alpha
Anticipate roadblocks, structure problem-solving, and support the CRO and leadership team in prioritizing high-impact goals
Gather insights and synthesize technical and operational data into clear narratives that guide strategic choices

green_flags: Clear reporting line to the CRO with high strategic visibility
Virtual Stock Option Plan included in benefits
Hybrid working model with flexible hours
Comprehensive benefits package (30 days vacation, wellness, mental health, bike lease, pension, transport subsidy)
Role explicitly described as 'high-impact' with a well-articulated scope
red_flags: No specific office location mentioned in the JD body — city/country unclear for candidates assessing relocation
Years of experience not quantified ('extensive' is vague, making self-assessment difficult)
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Hybrid office time expectation not specified (percentage or days not stated)

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'extensive experience' and strategic scope; no explicit level or years stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as European sovereign AI provider)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)

compensation.source set to 'unknown' — no salary data of any kind provided
ambiguities: The JD title says 'Chief of Staff to the CRO' but the body alternates between describing the CRO as 'Chief Research Officer' — both readings are consistent; treated as Chief Research Officer throughout.
No office location is named anywhere in the JD; given company is Aleph Alpha (headquartered in Heidelberg, Germany), role is likely Germany-based but this is an inference, not stated.

Language requirement says 'Fluency in English required; fluency in German is a strong plus' — German classified as nice-to-have (soft) per JD language, but given likely Germany location it may effectively be harder in practice.
